Acne.rationale: Salicylic acid (BHA) unclogs pores; benzoyl peroxide kills acne bacteria; adapalene/tretinoin normalises cell turnover; niacinamide reduces sebum and inflammation; azelaic acid is antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory.

Hyperpigmentation.rationale: Vitamin C inhibits tyrosinase (the enzyme that produces melanin); niacinamide blocks melanosome transfer; alpha arbutin and kojic acid are tyrosinase inhibitors; tretinoin accelerates cell renewal to shed pigmented cells.

Pores.rationale: Salicylic acid dissolves the sebum and debris that stretches pores; niacinamide tightens pore walls and regulates oil production; glycolic acid exfoliates the surface to reduce their appearance.

Fine Lines.rationale: Tretinoin and retinol are the only topical ingredients with gold-standard RCT evidence for stimulating collagen and reducing wrinkle depth; peptides signal fibroblasts to produce collagen; vitamin C is a cofactor in collagen synthesis; hyaluronic acid provides immediate plumping hydration.

Texture.rationale: Chemical exfoliation with AHAs (glycolic, lactic) and BHA (salicylic) smooths rough surface texture by dissolving the bonds between dead skin cells; tretinoin normalises keratinisation for long-term refinement.

Dryness.rationale: Hyaluronic acid attracts and holds water in the skin; ceramides seal the lipid barrier to prevent trans-epidermal water loss; lactic acid is a natural moisturising factor (NMF) component.

Oiliness.rationale: Niacinamide suppresses sebum production; salicylic acid penetrates the follicle to clear excess oil; azelaic acid normalises keratinisation and reduces abnormal sebum output.

Sensitivity.rationale: Niacinamide calms redness and reinforces the barrier; ceramides restore disrupted lipid layers; hyaluronic acid hydrates without irritants; azelaic acid is anti-inflammatory and well-tolerated by sensitive skin.
